JM and 5M have insane range, but can leave you vulnerable, so be careful with that. You'd have to use like JL for less committal pokes to help with moon jumps and stuff. He has 3M, 2M, 1M, 3M reaching the furthest, which can be good for landing snipes if you're very accurate, and they can combo into each other. They kind of change trajectory when you're closer, which can be strong with movement, especially dashing momentum 2s plus assist for mix from neutral. Being able to move with Kibu is key to maximize his pokes and assist calls to help him out. On the ground, he has candy beam that isn't too good as a big committ small beam. In the air though, this turns into the better normal beam attack that's much better, much bigger, too, and safer and can be super dash. While 214s is more for pressure, I have seen good players use it in tandem with assist to lock them down and get some mix from full screen away. He also has his mystic ball setups to quickly change aerial positioning. It's actually kind of nice as a whiff punish tool. J2H helps to stall in the air, too. And he has a command grab with 214 into his inputs. This is useful for very specific situations, especially if they keep lingering in this aerial area close to you. That EX one can be comboed with 5L afterwards. His mixups are insane. First of all, with his normals, it's interesting how he can do double to triple lows with 2M 1M or 3M, 2M 1M being the unskilled starter instead of 2M, funnily enough, which also brings them out the corner, which can be great in spark for instant cross-ups. and his left rights are interesting because his JS can be an instant cross up or the same side converted with his ball for combo. Medium ball needing to hold back on it. So 236M holding four. So speaking of holding back, it does give you a small gap, but holding back makes it plus two on that medium version. Even on the EX one where you hold back on it, but you can't really extend with a normal since 2 can counter you. Although key blast afterwards can help to beat their down heavies. His mystic ball can be interesting to use after his two mind games to overextend or ex balls to counter, especially right after the first hit of 5M to get momentum on your tools. If you throw an assist after his mystic ball, that's when you can get a high low mix 50/50 with a low hitting 2L. And whenever you go for a high with Kibu, whether IDs are on mixups, you can get triple overheads with JLMH because the medium stalls him, which allows for the heavy to land. from higher heights. You can even get quadruple overheads with LMLH since only one hit of the medium hits overhead, which can also mix in with whiffing the heavy for fake out into lows as well. A bit riskier mix is with 2S plus assist. Doing it after 5M gives you momentum where you can get an easier high low, which is more ambiguous depending on your assist as well. But guard cancel reflect does phase through this. Although I do like it because you can go into 2S nothing to bait those out. So purposefully doing 2S to make them reflect where you do nothing for huge damage on the bait. Going into 5M instead of that manual 1 for unskilled starters. And his iconic mix comes from assist into 214s. Then you hold up to float or down to fast fall. I've actually seen people throw out 214s as solo in pressure with good tempo to abuse respect or to throw off different timings even on his level three. His level three in general doesn't give much solid mix though. So that a assist the same as his arm ball giving 50/50 setups to every character because of that huge 57 frames of blocks and as an A assist. Make sure you get the timing right on these though. You have to call it earlier because of his huge startup. This can also be used for interesting multi-hitting combo extensions as well with the right timing. B is a lot easier to use as a short range larid assist since it's much faster to come out, but only at 32 frames of block. C is just his beam, but you can angle it up to aim higher by holding up plus the assist. Having 32 frames of blocks on an easy combos like usual for a regular C assist. Synergy wise, Ku is interesting because you want to find assist that can help him in his neutral patterns and mix ups at the same time. So, flexibility to be used in different situations is best, especially if they can cover angles that he normally can't. But, he can usually mix with a lot of assist anyway. So, you want to focus on that neutral aspect. If he's not optimal, having assists to help him with combo extensions are great, although he is pretty self-sufficient with combos. Assist can help with just his combo enders, especially if they're grounded, though. Check characters who can use his assist though, like his fast B1 or using his A assist if you need that mix for real or for combos and stuff. He's able to combo and convert very well while going corner to corner while preserving smash. So, it can help those who can use that smash later on or something like that. His level one is interesting. some supers that can keep them in that falling beam animation to add on to damage or different links for supers. But it also gives some time for other setups as well like whiffing it or getting it to some OP potential which can even apply to his aerial super if done high enough like in this example. So that can be fun you know but not really necessary for combos. A simple extension is with 2S J M LL J2S into the rest. So instead of jump canceling, you replace jump cancel with 2s on the ground. Neutral wise, he has solid pokes, but his teleports are his flashiest tools, making it great to escape and move around defensively since he can go in any direction, even offensively. And you can also use double teleports at by just holding the S button. Great to escape the corner, to avoid super dashes, pokes, etc. Especially with assist to help you poke as well. His Q blast also have a tracking nature comboing with a J2L or J2M overheads but it is a bit committal keeping you still. That's why it's best as a whiff punish tool because he's one of the only one that can whip punish anything from anywhere since this key blast tracks. You can angle these key blasts with different inputs like 6s to appear behind him which is really strong because that can help to link into his specials. It hits him from behind to bring them back down to the ground to hit jumps. So this is his most important key blast angle especially strong with EX slash as it frame traps everything when they get frustrated and super dash. Well, his J2ish is unique in that it has anti-air properties in the air to counter that and aerial approaches. So you want to bait them into super dashes with these on top of his level three being the only character with an arrow kid J2. So on top of void punishing, he's great at defensively managing air approaches. His most important neutral tool is his 22s projectile counter. 4 in one counter to all projectiles and assist where he armors up invincible to all as he fires a counter blast to hit. That counter is super plus on block too. great to counter a lot of things being an anti-zoner gaining a turn from everything since it's very very hard to manage once Zanemba enters this counter state even supers and grabs can't stop him but there are ways to like back dash out of it if you're very precise probably his main attractor great to manage neutral and game mechanics his other specials are cool with orbs that can detonate if you input that same button that activated it especially the AX one by pressing 214H and then detonating with a heavy they disappear if you get hit but he has his teleports and stuff right to avoid you This can lock down the ground, especially using your movement to lead them into the orb to catch landings, give you plus frames on hit, or to converse stray hits if you time that perfectly, but damn, that's some mastery. This can sometimes increase anxiety in opponents to enhance your base, so be raised to punish. Holding up when summoning the orbs keeps them higher up in pressure. His tools are really good. No lows besides 5 LL and 2M, but has good rhythm otherwise. Something tricky is how his auto combo counts as a crossup. JL makes for very fast overhead cross-ups which combos with his EX if done right.
